finding: macro body byte-identical to the pre-extraction ziee form except the external-crate path-qualification (T-1); the emitted RepositoryFactory/Deref-wrappers/ReposAccessor/Repos/init_repositories tokens are unchanged, so the generated API in ziee is identical

the declare_repositories! invocation + its 33-entry list are verbatim; only the macro DEF + 3 now-dead use lines were removed and a `use ziee_framework::declare_repositories;` added — Repos still generated in core::repository
E8 golden: ui+desktop types.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL and openapi.json canonically-equal (jq -S) vs baseline — Repos is process-internal, not wire-relevant; generated files restored via git checkout

#[macro_export] macro resolves hard-coded paths in the INVOKING crate; all external deps fully-qualified (::once_cell/::sqlx/::std/::paste/::tracing) so resolution does not depend on ziee's use-imports; $module_path/$type/$field are invocation-substituted so crate:: still points at ziee

caller-owned emitted item names ($type::new, get_factory(), RepositoryFactory, <Type>Repos, Repos) left UNQUALIFIED on purpose — they are defined by the same expansion in ziee; qualifying them would be wrong. Verified by successful expansion + build

the 3 use lines (once_cell/sqlx/std::sync::Arc) were used ONLY by the macro body; after self-qualification they are dead and were removed to avoid -D unused-imports. ziee builds with zero new warnings

ziee-framework gains only `pub mod repository;` (a macro of pure tokens, no new deps); the framework holds the GENERATOR, never a Repos instance — no framework-facing global introduced; skeleton-server still builds

`pub use repository::{Repos, init_repositories, is_repos_initialized};` unchanged — the re-exported symbols are still generated in core::repository, so the module's public surface + all downstream import paths are identical
#[cfg(not(test))] on the init_repositories re-init warning still keys off the INVOKING crate's test cfg (ziee) — same crate as pre-move, so warning-in-prod / silent-in-test behavior is unchanged
ziee-desktop consumes ziee's generated Repos (does not invoke the macro itself); the single ziee-side expansion serves both binaries. cargo check -p ziee-desktop exit 0

no #[cfg(test)] tests existed in the pre-move repository.rs; none moved, none dropped (A5 shrink-guard: no covering test id removed). The macro is exercised transitively by every integration test that touches Repos

the generated Repos const's doc-comment example kept its plain ``` doctest fence verbatim (not changed to `ignore`) so the tokens emitted into ziee are byte-identical to pre-move — no new/changed ziee doctest
cargo check --workspace on the SDK is clean; the macro file is not type-checked in ziee-framework (no invocation there), so it needs no deps of its own and raises no lint
